pom.xml.general_idea: Update the target Java version property from 11 to 17 to align with the JDK migration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run as Java 11 code, failing to utilize Java 17 features or enforce Java 17 compatibility checks.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the unified release flag for modern Java compilation.
pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer compiler plugin versions and Java releases; using them may cause warnings or errors, and they do not enforce module path consistency like the release flag does.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Maven compiler plugin version to a snapshot release compatible with Java 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older compiler plugin versions may not fully support Java 17 language features or the release flag correctly, potentially leading to compilation failures or incorrect bytecode generation.
